role_summary: Architect of the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee from SCLC's Atlanta basement in 1960; long-tenured NAACP organizer; the structural intelligence behind multiple civil rights organizations across five decades.
ai_reading: Among the corpus's 136 interviewees the embedding finds Baker discussed across eight separate transcripts spanning NAACP staff (Mildred Bond Roxborough, entry 90), SNCC organizers (Timothy Jenkins entry 120, Cleveland Sellers entry 22, Reginald Robinson entry 101, Lawrence Guyot entry 76, Charles McLaurin entry 17, Judy Richardson entry 69), and SCLC leadership (Joseph Echols Lowery entry 66). The breadth of the discussion across mutually-distant subgroups is the catalog's clearest pattern of corpus-wide structuring presence for any external figure tracked here: she is named more across distinct interview chapters than several figures whose cultural fame exceeds hers.
biographical_paragraph: Ella Baker is discussed in this corpus by 8 interviewees across the catalog (Mildred Bond Roxborough (entry 90), Timothy Jenkins (entry 120), Dr. Cleveland Sellers (entry 22), Reginald Robinson (entry 101), Joseph Echols Lowery (entry 66), Lawrence Guyot (entry 76), Judy Richardson (entry 69), Charles McLaurin (entry 17)), but was not interviewed for the Civil Rights History Project; this catalog page treats Ella Baker as an external figure whose presence in the archive is mediated through other voices. The canonical scholarly source the catalog cites for biographical orientation appears as [src: 1] below.

sources.title: Barbara Ransby, Ella Baker and the Black Freedom Movement: A Radical Democratic Vision
sources.publisher: University of North Carolina Press, 2003; WorldCat OCLC 51046011
